name: Pinned Crossfade

params.name: hold
params.describe: how long the section stays pinned, in viewport heights. Clamped 1.5-3 so a bad value can never trap the reader.

license: MIT (@bsmnt/scrollytelling (c) 2023 basement.studio - LICENSE file verified) + GSAP under GreenSock standard license, free for all commercial use since Webflow Apr 2025. Same rail as kinetic-text-split.
notes: Two-panel pinned crossfade on the proven @bsmnt/scrollytelling rail (same as kinetic-text-split). Panel one dissolves into panel two while the section is pinned, scrubbed to scroll. Ships a hard fallback: prefers-reduced-motion OR touch renders both panels as a plain vertical stack with no pin and no fade (never pin on touch). Pin distance clamped 1.5-3vh; crossfade window fixed at 30-70% so neither panel is ever stuck half-legible; only opacity animates. Both tweens use ease:'none' because scroll IS the clock - gsap's default power1.out eases on top of the scrub and measured 25/75 at the scroll midpoint; linear gives an exact 50/50. Verified live across 21 sampled positions in the pinned range: opacity A+B sums to 1.00 at every one (no dip to background, no double-exposure) and the pin holds top=0 throughout. Ships a stale-position guard: any document image still pending at mount gets a settle listener that schedules one trailing ScrollTrigger.refresh(), so a late-loading image above the section (generated imagery renders on demand) cannot leave the pin/crossfade holding stale positions - verified live: an 800px late image shifted the trigger start/end by exactly +800 with no manual refresh, and the crossfade then read exactly 50/50 at the refreshed midpoint. NON-video by design - a pinned video is the documented low-end-hardware failure (PP Neue Montreal critique).
